title: Wave speed
short: How fast a wave's crests travel, v = λ·f, measured in m/s.
full:
Wave speed is how fast a disturbance — a crest, a pulse — moves through a medium, v = λ·f. It is set by the medium, not by the source: sound travels at about 340 m/s in air whatever its pitch, and light at c ≈ 3 × 10⁸ m/s in vacuum.

Because v = λ·f is fixed by the medium, raising the frequency shortens the wavelength in proportion.

The SI unit is metres per second (m/s).
